title: D.C. FOIA Request: Telecommunication Logs and Metadata for DISB Investigator Mark Pendleton (Jan 1, 2022 – Feb 28, 2022)

communications.subject: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: D.C. FOIA Request: Telecommunication Logs and Metadata for DISB Investigator Mark Pendleton (Jan 1, 2022 – Feb 28, 2022)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the Designated FOIA Officers for DISB and OCTO:

Under the District of Columbia Freedom of Information Act (D.C. Code §§ 2-531—539), I am formally requesting the production of the following specific server-level telecommunication records:

1. Inbound and Outbound Call Logs: Any and all telecommunication logs, billing records, or server-level metadata detailing inbound and outbound phone calls for the government-issued desk phone and/or government-issued mobile device assigned to DISB Investigator Mark Pendleton.
2. Timeframe: The strict chronological window of January 1, 2022, through February 28, 2022.
3. Required Data Fields: The requested logs must include, at a minimum: 
    * The date of each call.
    * The exact timestamp (start time) of each call.
    * The duration of each call.
    * The originating and terminating phone numbers (subject to valid redactions outlined below).

Clarifications to Prevent Improper Denial or Delay:

* This is NOT a request for an investigative case file. I am strictly requesting the server-level telecommunication metadata generated by the carrier/provider and maintained by the District of Columbia. Therefore, any claim that a specific "investigative file" was closed, deleted, or cannot be located is wholly irrelevant to the existence of these overarching municipal telecommunication billing records.
* Redaction Protocol: I acknowledge that under D.C. FOIA privacy exemptions, the agency may redact the specific, unlisted phone numbers of private citizens. However, the metadata itself (date, time, duration) cannot be redacted under this exemption. Furthermore, telephone numbers belonging to commercial entities, law firms, registered businesses, or other government agencies are not subject to personal privacy exemptions and must remain fully unredacted.

If this request is denied in whole or in part, please provide a detailed justification citing the specific statutory exemption under D.C. Code § 2-534 being invoked for each individual redaction or withheld record. 

Please provide these records in a digital spreadsheet or standard PDF format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FRED GAMBLE JR

title: FBI: Juris Upatnieks

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI: Juris Upatnieks
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting all records, files, dossiers, intelligence reports, correspondence, memoranda, and documentation maintaining a reference to or concerning the individual identified below:
Full Name: Juris Upatnieks
Date of Birth: May 7, 1936  
Place of Birth: Riga, Latvia  
Date of Death: May 17, 2026  
Key Historic Associations: Pioneer of laser holography, researcher at Willow Run Laboratories, the University of Michigan's Institute of Science and Technology, and the Environmental Research Institute of Michigan (ERIM).  

As the subject of this request is deceased as of May 17, 2026, privacy exemptions under 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(7)(C) and (b)(6) regarding personal privacy are severely diminished. I have attached proof of death (obituary/public records documentation) to satisfy bureaucratic verification requirements. The death of Upatnieks was widely reported in the media:
https://eng.lsm.lv/article/society/education/26.05.2026-distinguished-scientist-juris-upatnieks-has-died.a648805/

Scope of Search & Explicit Directives:

Please conduct a thorough search of the Central Records System (CRS), including all main files and cross-references, electronic surveillance (ELSUR) indices, and any localized field office files (specifically looking at the Detroit and Cleveland Field Offices due to his historical residency and research hubs in Ann Arbor and Akron).
Furthermore, please execute the following parameters:

Please include a search of any records or documents understood to be, or colloquially referred to as "COINTELPRO records" under the bureau's custody that could be construed as responsive to a request for records related to Juris Upatnieks.

As you know, the Federal Bureau of Investigation is required to search the records stored at NARA’s Washington Records Center in Suitland, MD, but still technically under the FBI’s control. For more information see 36 CFR 1250.8. Please also conduct a physical search of records at NARA's Washington Records Center in Suitland for any records or documents related to Juris Upatnieks.

If the bureau cannot fulfill certain parts of this request or if certain portions must be redacted, please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank for the work FBI does every day, and thank you for the bureau's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: FBI: Merced civic leader Miller Alva Parker

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI: Merced civic leader Miller Alva Parker
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request copies of any and all records, files, memoranda, correspondence, reports, index references, administrative notations, surveillance records, investigative records, cross-references, or other materials maintained by the Federal Bureau of Investigation relating to:

Miller Alva Parker
Born: September 18, 1913 – Arkansas
Died: August 27, 1988 – Merced, California

Mr. Parker served in the United States Army during World War II from approximately 1942 to 1945. He was assigned to Ninth Headquarters in Europe and was discharged with the rank of Master Sergeant. Following World War II, he became a civic and business leader in Merced, California.

A copy of his 1940 draft registration card is attached to assist in identification and records searches. A story about this death ran in the Monday, August 29th, 1988 edition of the Merced Sun Star Newspaper. 

I request that the FBI conduct a comprehensive search of all indices and record systems reasonably likely to contain responsive records, including but not limited to:

Central Records System (CRS)
Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) indices
Cross-reference (“see” and “see also”) files
Administrative files
Field office records
Security index records
Any retired paper records or microfilm holdings
Any files transferred to archival storage but remaining under FBI control

Please include a search of any records or documents understood to be, or colloquially referred to as "COINTELPRO records" under the bureau's custody that could be construed as responsive to a request for records related to Miller Alva Parker.

As you know, the Federal Bureau of Investigation is required to search the records stored at NARA’s Washington Records Center in Suitland, MD, but still technically under the FBI’s control. For more information see 36 CFR 1250.8.

Please also conduct a physical search of records at NARA's Washington Records Center in Suitland for any records or documents related to Miller Alva Parker.

In addition, I request that the FBI search for any “main files” as well as any references where Mr. Parker appears only incidentally, including name-reference entries, routing slips, communication logs, or enclosures to larger investigative files.

If any responsive records have been destroyed, transferred, or otherwise removed from FBI custody, I request any available documentation describing the disposition of those records, including destruction schedules, transfer documentation, or accession information.

Because the subject of this request is deceased, and died in 1988, privacy concerns should be substantially diminished. If proof of death is required, I am willing to provide supporting documentation.

If the agency cannot fulfill certain parts of this request or if certain portions must be redacted, please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: LexisNexis and ALPR Contracting Documents - Hamet PD (CA)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: LexisNexis and ALPR Contracting Documents - Hamet PD (CA)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

On May 20th, the DEA and IRS-CI filed a statement in United States v. Pham [4:26-mj-04166, (D. Mass. May 20, 2026) ECF No. 95 - https://www.courtlistener.com/docket/73348335/95/1/united-states-v-pham/]. This document indicated that:
A) Federal agents have access to local Automated License Plate Recognition (ALPR) systems operating in the city of Hamet
B) The ALPR system that federal agents have access to appear to be mobile (mounted on vehicles)
C) Federal agents have access to police records that show prior contacts at a local Hamet address

To better understand this filing by federal agents, we are requesting the following documents:
1) Is your agency contributing to the Public Safety Data Exchange? 
 - Documents: Any signed contracts, NDAs, Schedule A, or addendums between Hamet Police and LexisNexis (including LexisNexis Risk Solutions, Accurint, AVCC, etc.). This includes documentation on what data you provide to LexisNexis for them to host your CommunityCrimeMap.
2) Is Hamet PD part of a law enforcement consortium?
 - Documents: Any MOUs or other document that your agency signed to join a regional law enforcement consortium (if any)
3) What local ALPRs in Hamet were federal agents querying for their investigation, and were the systems run by Hamet PD or private? 
 - Documents: Any ALPR policy, ALPR training manual, or contract agreement with a vendor (Flock, Motorola/Vigilant, etc.) that would provide insight into what systems are present, AND how they are shared (i.e. does local data flow into PSDEX/Accurint?) 

Written statements are sufficient to fulfil this request - however some agencies choose to only reply with documents, so I provided the relevant documents requested as well. I am not seeking active case information or PII. I am only seeking to understand in what way local law enforcement data and citizen location info is available to federal agencies.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: Obama Presidential Library: Request for Records Concerning Ebola Preparedness, Central and West Africa Surveillance Infrastructure, and 2016–2017 Presidential Transition Warnings

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Request for Records Concerning Ebola Preparedness, Central and West Africa Surveillance Infrastructure, and 2016–2017 Presidential Transition Warnings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records specifically related to Ebola virus disease preparedness, the sustainability of U.S.-supported Ebola surveillance networks in Africa, and warnings or risk assessments generated during the 2016–2017 presidential transition. 
This request is intentionally scoped to Ebola and Africa. It does not seek records related to pandemic influenza, HIV/AIDS, coronaviruses, or other infectious disease programs except insofar as those records are inextricably co-mingled with Ebola-specific assessments.

Records Requested:

All records — including memoranda, briefing books, risk assessments, talking points, slide presentations, cables, emails, interagency communications, draft reports, annexes, and "not for distribution" materials — created, received, or compiled between January 1, 2014 and April 30, 2017, pertaining to the following:

A. Presidential Transition Warnings — Ebola Specific

All transition memoranda, briefing binders, or handoff documents prepared by NSC staff (including the Directorate for Global Health Security and Biodefense), CDC, USAID, or the Department of Defense, warning the incoming Trump Administration about risks related to:

The discontinuation, draw down or disruption of Ebola surveillance programs in Africa
Loss of institutional knowledge or field relationships built during the 2014–2016 West Africa response
Funding cliffs for Ebola-specific preparedness programs for FY 2018


Documents containing the keywords: "Ebola transition," "Ebola status quo," "Ebola briefing," "incoming administration Ebola," "Trump Ebola"," "Trump transition Ebola," "continuity Ebola," "Ebola program sustainability," "post-EVD response," or "Ebola preparedness handoff."

B. Sustainability and Structural Warnings — Africa Field Networks

Assessments, program reviews, or internal evaluations addressing the long-term sustainability of U.S.-supported Ebola surveillance and response infrastructure in:

The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC)
Republic of Congo
Central African Republic
Guinea, Sierra Leone, or Liberia (post-2016 drawdown assessments)
The broader Congo Basin region


Records addressing whether field networks, contact tracing capacity, and community health worker programs built during and after the 2014–2016 outbreak were contractor-driven, temporary, or structurally dependent on continued U.S. funding — and what would happen if that funding ceased.

Documents containing the keywords: "Ebola surveillance gap," "Congo Basin Ebola," "DRC Ebola risk," "field network drawdown," "ring vaccination sustainability," "contact tracing capacity," "zoonotic spillover Ebola," "bat reservoir surveillance," "Ebola blind spots," "host country Ebola capacity," or "Ebola program withdrawal."

C. After-Action Reviews with Forward-Looking Risk Projections

After-action reviews, lessons-learned reports, or retrospective analyses from the 2014–2016 West Africa Ebola response that contain explicit forward-looking risk assessments, including:

Projections of future Ebola resurgence in Central or West Africa
Recommendations that were not implemented or were abandoned
Warnings about surveillance blind spots in the DRC or Congo Basin
Evaluations of USAID's response 
Evaluations of the State Department's (and in particular specific US embassies and their social media accounts) communication strategy regarding the Ebola response  
USAID or State Department analyses of the consequences of reducing Ebola-related foreign assistance in Africa
Any documents assessing Ebola preparedness as a national security matter


Documents containing the keywords: "Ebola recurrence," "USAID Ebola," "Ebola resurgence risk," "future outbreak," "lessons learned Ebola," "unimplemented recommendations," "abandoned continuity plan," or "EVD risk projection."

EXPLICIT EXCLUSIONS
To assist in scoping and processing, the requester explicitly does not seek records primarily concerning:

Pandemic influenza or avian influenza (H5N1, H7N9, or related strains)
HIV/AIDS programs
SARS, MERS, or coronavirus preparedness
Zika, dengue, or mosquito-borne illness programs
General Global Health Security Agenda (GHSA) records not specifically mentioning Ebola or filovirus preparedness

If a responsive document addresses Ebola alongside these other topics, the requester requests that the Ebola-relevant portions be produced even if other sections are withheld or redacted on other grounds.

Thank you to the Obama Library and their commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Request for Records Concerning Ebola Preparedness, Central and West Africa Surveillance Infrastructure, and 2016–2017 Presidential Transition Warnings
communications.communication:
Dear Robert Delaware,

Thank you for contacting the Barack Obama Presidential Library. Please see 
the attached information concerning your FOIA request.

Please be aware that our collections are from the Obama presidential 
administration (1/20/2009 to 1/20/2017). Questions about records from 
January 21, 2017 through April, 30, 2017 can be addressed to 
trump.library@nara.gov. 

Please also be aware that the Department of Defense, CDC, and USAID are 
separate agencies whose records may not be captured in the Obama 
Administration records, which are preserved by the National Archives. For 
access to such records, you will need to be submitted a FOIA directly to 
each of them. Please go to https://www.foia.gov/ and select "create a 
request" from the top menu, which will guide you through the process.
 
Please be sure to notify me of any change in email address as any email 
returned undeliverable will result in your case being closed.
 
Sincerely,
 
Sarah Cain
Archivist & FOIA Coordinator
Barack Obama Presidential Library

title: Offices of Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees

communications.subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Offices of Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Via Email and U.S. Mail
Records Custodian
Offices of Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees
4 State Police Plaza Drive
Little Rock, AR 72209
Tel: (501) 324-9493

Re: Arkansas FOIA Request (A.C.A. § 25‑19‑101 et seq.)
    Cross County Jail and City of Wynne detention/holding facilities — inspections, staffing approvals, deficiencies, complaints, and related records (2017–present)

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act (A.C.A. § 25‑19‑101 et seq.), I request access to and electronic copies of the public records described below for the period January 1, 2017 to the present concerning:

• Cross County Detention Center/Jail (Wynne, Arkansas), and
• Any City of Wynne detention facilities or spaces (including any “city jail,” book‑in/24‑hour/overnight facilities, and temporary holding cells within the Wynne Police Department) (collectively, “Wynne PD detention/holding”).

This request is made in the public interest and relates to compliance with the Arkansas Criminal Detention Facility Standards and matters of significant community concern.

Requested Records (2017–present)

1) Inspections, findings, and transmittals (Chapter 16)
   • All onsite inspection reports, inspection checklists, findings, deficiency notices, corrective action plans, and follow‑up reports regarding Cross County Jail and Wynne PD detention/holding.
   • All transmittal letters/reports sent pursuant to § 16‑1003 (e.g., to the Chief Circuit Judge, Chief Executive, county judge, and governing body).
   • Any site visit notes, photographs, and inspector correspondence related to these facilities.
   • Any fire marshal inspection records or Department of Health food service inspection documents contained within your inspection files (see §§ 14‑1002, 12‑1003).

2) Annual staffing approvals (Chapter 9)
   • All annual staffing pattern submissions and approvals for Cross County Jail and any Wynne PD detention/holding areas, as required by § 9‑1001(A).
   • All correspondence (emails/letters/memos) between your Office/Committees and these facilities regarding staffing levels, awake/alert coverage, required visual checks, cross‑gender supervision, and emergency procedures (see §§ 9‑1001 to 9‑1002).

3) Policies reviewed or referenced in inspection files (Chs. 3, 5–15)
   For each inspection file, any copies of—or notes referencing—the following policies reviewed for Cross County Jail and Wynne PD detention/holding:
   • Intake/receiving, verification of detainment, jail logs, personal property handling, strip searches (Chapter 6; §§ 6‑1001 to 6‑1007, 6‑1013).
   • Detainee rights and disciplinary procedures (Chapter 7; §§ 7‑1001 to 7‑1003).
   • Classification and separation (Chapter 8; §§ 8‑1001 to 8‑1003).
   • Security policies: cross‑gender supervision; emergency plans for escapes/riots/fire/medical; contraband searches; key control; work‑release security (Chapter 9; § 9‑1002).
   • Medical/dental/mental health; sick call and emergency procedures; medication administration; AED/first‑aid and life‑saving certifications; detox protocols; restraints (Chapter 10; §§ 10‑1001 to 10‑1015).
   • Mail/communication/visitation (Chapter 11).
   • Food service and dietician review references (Chapter 12).
   • Safety: fire/emergency plans, training, firefighting equipment, drills (Chapter 14).
   • Detainee services/exercise where applicable (Chapter 15).
   If your Office retains only checklists or notes indicating whether required policies were produced, absent, or deficient, please provide those records.

4) New construction/remodeling plan reviews and approvals (Chapter 17)
   • All plan review submissions, narratives of compliance, coordinator comments, and Committee approval letters for any new construction or remodeling at Cross County Jail or Wynne PD detention/holding areas since 2017 (see §§ 17‑1002 to 17‑1004).
   • Any deviations/variances requested and related correspondence.

5) Temporary holding/Book‑In/Overnight facilities oversight (Chapter 18)
   • Records identifying whether Wynne PD operates a Book‑In facility, Twenty‑Four Hour/Overnight facility, or Holding Cells as defined in Chapter 18.
   • Any inspections, review materials, deficiency notices, or corrective actions specific to such units, including compliance with § 18‑1003 (toilet access, staff observation, and food/water for detentions exceeding four hours).

6) Records retention and documentation notes (Chapter 6)
   • Any inspection findings or notes concerning recordkeeping compliance, including jail logs (§ 6‑1003), critical incident documentation (§ 6‑1012), medical/dental treatment records (§§ 10‑1004, 10‑1014), detainee disciplinary files (§ 6‑1011), personal property receipts (§ 6‑1005), and strip search documentation (§ 6‑1007).

7) Complaints, inquiries, and referrals
   • All complaints, tips, or inquiries received by your Office/Committees regarding Cross County Jail and Wynne PD detention/holding (2017–present), whether from detainees, family members, attorneys, courts, or the public.
   • Any resulting investigative notes, site visit notes, correspondence with the facilities, and referrals to prosecutors or other authorities.

8) Committee/Coordinator records referencing these facilities
   • Meeting agendas, minutes, memoranda, or summaries of the Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees or Coordinator that reference Cross County Jail or Wynne PD detention/holding, including entries regarding inspections, deficiencies, corrective actions, or noncompliance.

9) Wynne Police Department detention/holding areas (clarification and confirmation)
   • If the Wynne Police Department operates any detention/holding spaces (e.g., temporary holding cells, Book‑In facilities, or Twenty‑Four Hour/Overnight facilities), please provide:
     – All inspection reports, inspection checklists, findings, deficiency notices, corrective action plans, and follow‑up reports referencing Wynne PD detention/holding areas (2017–present).
     – All annual staffing pattern submissions and approvals referencing Wynne PD detention/holding areas, including cross‑gender supervision and visual check requirements (see §§ 9‑1001 to 9‑1002).
     – Any correspondence (emails/letters/memos) between your Office/Committees and Wynne PD or City of Wynne officials concerning these detention/holding areas, including classification under Chapter 18 and compliance with § 18‑1003 (toilets, observation, and food/water for detentions over four hours).
   • If your Office has no responsive records because Wynne PD does not operate such spaces, please confirm that in writing and identify any agency you believe maintains oversight records for Wynne PD detainee holding.

Production Format and Fees
• Please produce records electronically:
  – PDFs should be text‑searchable (OCR) and include any attachments.
  – Audio/video/photo files should be provided in native or standard playable formats with available metadata.
• If volume is large, please produce on a rolling basis as records are located.
• I request a fee waiver or reduction due to the considerable public interest in detention standards compliance and civil rights. Please advise in advance if anticipated copying costs exceed $50.

Withholdings and Redactions
If any records are withheld or redacted, please:
• Identify the specific AFOIA exemption(s) relied upon for each withholding.
• Provide a redaction/withholding log describing the record type, date, author/recipient (if not exempt), and general subject.
• Release all reasonably segregable non‑exempt portions as AFOIA requires.

Acknowledgment and Timing

Please acknowledge this request promptly and provide access to responsive records within the timelines set by A.C.A. § 25‑19‑105. If records are in active use or storage, please provide a good‑faith schedule for their availability. If any item is unclear or unduly burdensome, please contact me to discuss reasonable narrowing that preserves the scope and purpose of this request.

Thank you for your prompt attention. See Federal Court Case for verification of being a state resident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

3) If your agency contributes data to AVCC, PSDEX, or a local law enforcement data warehouse, please provide the field names that you contribute. Responsive documents may include API documentation, XML documents/files, screenshot of configuration file, configuration instructions, or other technical documents from LexisNexis to help with integrating RMS systems. 
4) Any meeting minutes, meeting media, or presentations from when Orange County Public School District agreed to contribute data to AVCC/PSDEX (if your agency does contribute)

I request records concerning legal review of the removal, disabling, discontinuation, archiving, or reduced public availability of National Climate Assessment materials, globalchange.gov, or other U.S. Global Change Research Program materials.
